name: Ancho-And Chipotle-Rubbed Pork Loin
recipeIngredient: 2 dried chipotle chilies (or 2 chipotles canned in)
1 tablespoon lard (you may substitute vegetable oil)
1 medium white onion, very thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the dried chilies in a preheated 200 degree oven for 3 minutes. Remove and open them.
- Discard seeds and stems.
2. Place the chilies in a bowl and cover with hot water.
3. After the chilies are soft (about 15 minutes), remove them from the water.
4. Add garlic, lard, cumin, cloves, salt, and pepper to the food processor. Pulse until a rough paste forms.
5. Rub the paste all over the pork loin.
6. Refrigerate for 8 hours.
7. Remove the pork from the refrigerator 1 hour before cooking.
8. In a clay pot, make a bed of half the sliced onion. Place the marinated pork on top and cover with remaining onions.
9. Follow your pot's instructions to set up the oven (usually soaking the lid).
10. Cook at 300 degrees for 1 hour without opening the pot.
11. Let the roast rest in its broth for 10 minutes before slicing thinly.
Note: The original text had minor formatting and punctuation issues, which have been corrected here.
